conversation.content:
You are playing a strategic game of trading resources with another player whose resources you have no knowledge about. 

RULES:
```

1. You can either:

A) Accept the trade by saying:
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

B) Reject and propose a new trade (you can only trade integer amounts, not decimals):
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... | Player BLUE Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... </newly proposed trade>

C) Don't accept or propose anything and wait for a new offer:

<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>


Note: the game will end if one of the players accepts. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only ACCEPT or NONE.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step by using the following format:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason>
Add as much text as you want. This information will not be sent to the other player. 
It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message> [add message] </message>
You can decide if you want disclose your resources and goals in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: X, Y
<my resources> X: 25, Y: 5 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<my name> Player RED </my name>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

More resources in general are always better.
This is the last round of trading. There are no future rounds after this one.
You are Player RED, start by making a proposal.

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<my resources> X: 25, Y: 5 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED with 25 units of X and 5 units of Y. My goal is to maximize total resources. Since I have a surplus of X and a deficit of Y, I must trade X for Y. As this is the last round, Player BLUE has no future leverage to reject a good deal. To ensure acceptance while securing Y, I will offer a generous rate of 10 X for 2 Y. This utilizes my surplus effectively. If BLUE has Y, this is likely a winning deal for them to offload Y, and I gain Y at a cost I can afford. However, if BLUE rejects, I will likely lower the X cost in subsequent proposals to 8 or 9 X for 2 Y to guarantee acceptance.</reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 25 X and 5 Y. Since this is the final round, you have no reason to hold out. I am offering to buy your Y with my surplus X. Let's swap 10 of my X for 2 of your Y. It is a fair deal given the circumstances. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 10 | Player BLUE Gives Y: 2 </newly proposed trade>

1. You can either:

Note: the game will end if one of the players accepts. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only ACCEPT or NONE.

3. You can reason step by step by using the following format:

<other player message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 25 X and 5 Y. Since this is the final round, you have no reason to hold out. I am offering to buy your Y with my surplus X. Let's swap 10 of my X for 2 of your Y. It is a fair deal given the circumstances. </other player message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 10 | Player BLUE Gives Y: 2 </other player proposed trade>

<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> The other player (RED) has 25 X and 5 Y. They are offering to trade 10 X for 2 Y. 
If I accept, I end up with:
X: 5 + 10 = 15
Y: 25 - 2 = 23
Total resources: 38.

If I reject and propose a better trade, I might get more.
Since RED has a surplus of X (25) and a deficit of Y (only 5), and I have a surplus of Y (25) and a deficit of X (only 5), the natural trade is X for Y.
The current offer is 5 X per 1 Y (10 X for 2 Y).
Given my scarcity of X, I should try to get as much X as possible for my Y.
I have 25 Y. I could potentially trade all of them if RED wants them enough.
RED has 25 X. If they trade all their X for some Y, the rate would be 1 X per 1 Y.
The current offer (5 X per 1 Y) is very favorable to me. Wait, no.
Current offer: I give 2 Y, get 10 X. That is 5 X per 1 Y.
If I trade 1 Y for 1 X, that is 1 X per 1 Y.
So the current offer is actually very good for me? Let's re-evaluate.
I value X more because I only have 5. RED has 25.
If I get 10 X for 2 Y, I have gained 10 X and lost 2 Y.
If I try to get more X, say 25 X for 5 Y?
That would be 5 X per 1 Y. Same rate.
What if I ask for 25 X for 5 Y? That is 5 X per 1 Y.
What if I ask for 25 X for 2 Y? That is 12.5 X per 1 Y.
RED has 25 X and 5 Y. They need Y.
If I offer to trade 5 Y for 25 X, the rate is 5 X per 1 Y.
If I offer to trade 2 Y for 25 X, the rate is 12.5 X per 1 Y.
RED might not agree to give 25 X for only 2 Y if they think 5 Y is fair for 25 X.
However, since this is the last round, RED wants to maximize their own total too.
RED currently has 25 X + 5 Y = 30.
If they trade 25 X for 5 Y, they end up with 5 X + 5 Y = 10. That's a huge loss for them.
So RED definitely does not want to give away all 25 X.
RED wants to keep some X.
Let's look at the current offer again: 10 X for 2 Y.
RED ends up with: 15 X + 3 Y = 18.
If I accept, I get 15 X + 23 Y = 38.
Is there a way for me to get more than 15 X?
I have 25 Y.
If I trade 5 Y, can I get 20 X? (Rate 4 X/Y).
If I trade 5 Y, can I get 25 X? (Rate 5 X/Y).
The current offer is 10 X for 2 Y -> 5 X per Y.
It seems the current offer is already quite good for me in terms of rate (5 X for 1 Y).
But I have 25 Y. If I trade 2 Y, I only get 10 X.
If I trade 5 Y, maybe I can get 25 X?
If RED trades 25 X for 5 Y, they end up with 5 X + 5 Y = 10.
If RED trades 15 X for 3 Y, they end up with 10 X + 2 Y = 12.
If RED trades 10 X for 2 Y, they end up with 15 X + 3 Y = 18.
It seems RED prefers to keep X.
The current offer gives RED 18 total resources.

<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

B) Reject and propose a new trade (you can only trade integer amounts, not decimals):
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... | Player BLUE Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... </newly proposed trade>

C) Don't accept or propose anything and wait for a new offer:

<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>


Note: the game will end if one of the players accepts. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only ACCEPT or NONE.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step by using the following format:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason>
Add as much text as you want. This information will not be sent to the other player. 
It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message> [add message] </message>
You can decide if you want disclose your resources and goals in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: X, Y
<my resources> X: 5, Y: 25 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<my name> Player BLUE </my name>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

More resources in general are always better.
This is the last round of trading. There are no future rounds after this one.
